response: In C# it is possible to measure the time it takes to send a UDP packet to a server but not the time it takes for the server to respond back. This is because UDP is connectionless and does not guarantee delivery or response. To measure the time taken to send the packet you can record the time before sending and then calculate the difference after the send operation completes. This gives you the time taken for the packet to be sent from your machine to the network. However this does not include the time it takes for the packet to reach the server. For a one way measurement you can use the Stopwatch class to track the duration of the send operation. This method will not provide the same accuracy as a ping which measures round trip time.
